zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady de Say於大約1255年出生在英國肯特羅徹斯特時，她的父親Sir William de Say III47歲，母親Sibyl Marshal44歲。她和John de Sudeley 1st Baron Sudeley至少有5個兒子和1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady de Say于大约1255年出生在英国肯特羅徹斯特时，她的父亲Sir William de Say III47岁，母亲Sibyl Marshal44岁。她和John de Sudeley 1st Baron Sudeley至少有5个儿子和1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Say
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

variants.name: Sey
meanings: English and Scottish: probably a variant of   Say   particularly prevalent in Scotland.
Dutch and Flemish: from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal name Sigiheri (see Seger ).
West African (Ghana): Akan (Fante) name of unexplained etymology.

variants.name: Sayer
meanings: English: common occupational name from Middle English sayer, a phonetic variant of Sawyer , which in Wales and neighboring English counties may have been reinforced by the synonymous Welsh saer ‘carpenter’ and everywhere by a possible borrowing into Middle English of Old French seieor, saieur ‘sawyer’.

English: rare occupational name perhaps denoting an assayer of metals, or a servant who tasted his lord's food before serving, from Middle English saiour ‘assayer, tester’ (a shortened form of Old French assaiour, ultimately from Late Latin exagium, a derivative of exagmināre ‘to weigh’).
English: rare occupational name for a professional reciter or minstrel, from Middle English seier(e) ‘speaker’.

variants.name: Saye
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady de Say was born about 1255, in Rochester, Kent, England, her father, Sir William de Say III, was 47 and her mother, Sibyl Marshal, was 44. She had at least 5 sons and 1 daughter with John de Sudeley 1st Baron Sudeley.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady de Say est née environ 1255, à Rochester, Kent, Angleterre, son père, Sir William de Say III, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Sibyl Marshal, avait 44 ans. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille avec John de Sudeley 1st Baron Sudeley.

de.lifeSummary: Als Lady de Say um 1255 in Rochester, Kent, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William de Say III 47 und ihre Mutter Sibyl Marshal 44. Sie hatte mit John de Sudeley 1st Baron Sudeley mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Lady de Say è nata il circa 1255 nel Rochester, Kent, England, sua padre, Sir William de Say III, aveva 47 anni e sua madre, Sibyl Marshal, aveva 44 anni. Lei avuto almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia con John de Sudeley 1st Baron Sudeley.

ja.lifeSummary: Lady de Sayが約1255年にイギリスケントロチェスターで生まれた時，父Sir William de Say IIIは47歳，母Sibyl Marshalは44歳であった。彼女はJohn de Sudeley 1st Baron Sudeleyとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Lady de Say(이)가 대략 1255년 영국 켄트 록헤스터에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William de Say III(은)는 47세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sibyl Marshal(은)는 44세였다. 그녀는 John de Sudeley 1st Baron Sudeley(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Lady de Say nasceu aproximadamente 1255, em Rochester, Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William de Say III, tinha 47 anos e sua mãe, Sibyl Marshal, tinha 44 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha com John de Sudeley 1st Baron Sudeley.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady de Say родилась около 1255 г. в местности рошестир, кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William de Say III было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Sibyl Marshal, 44. У неё было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь от John de Sudeley 1st Baron Sudeley.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady de Say nació aproximadamente 1255, en Rochester, Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William de Say III, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Sibyl Marshal, tenía 44 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija con John de Sudeley 1st Baron Sudeley.
